title: Md Al Amin
text:
Md Al Amin is an undergraduate student of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology at Jagannath University, Bangladesh, with strong training in both experimental and computational biomedical research. He currently works as a Research Assistant at the Red-Green Research Centre, focusing on peptide-based drug design using computational biology approaches, while also contributing to departmental laboratory projects.
In his departmental research, he investigates Bangladeshi plant-derived phytochemicals as eco-friendly larvicidal alternatives to conventional insecticides. This work aligns with the One Health and microbiome framework, promoting safer and sustainable public health interventions, with applications including mosquito repellents and nanoparticle formulations.
Beyond research, he has held leadership positions such as President of ISCB RSG Bangladesh and actively contributes to academic outreach and teaching programs.
He aims to integrate computational and experimental approaches in drug discovery and molecular biology, building a research career that bridges biology and computational science to advance translational biomedical innovation.

Key Contributions:
- Supports computational biology research projects.
- Creates tutorials and pipelines for protein modeling.
- Assists in training other students and researchers.
- Maintains reproducible code and workflow documentation

Key Takeaways:
- AlphaFold predicts protein structures with high accuracy.
- Useful for research in drug discovery, molecular biology, and bioinformatics.
- Free tools (AlphaFold DB and ColabFold) allow hands-on exploration.
- Understanding AlphaFold helps participants see the future of computational biology.

title: Rahnuma Tabassum
text:
Rahnuma Tabassum is an undergraduate student of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology at Jagannath University, Bangladesh, with strong training in both experimental and computational biomedical research.She worked as an internee in the GSA Bioinformatics Internship 2025, organized by GNOBB, SPSB, and ASI School of Life in collaboration with Chiral Bangladesh and DeepBio Limited, demonstrating outstanding dedication and excellence.
Rahnuma showcased her expertise in computational biology through her research titled “Integrated Transcriptomic Meta-Analysis Identifies Pathway Crosstalk and Therapeutic Targets in Liver Hepatocellular Carcinoma.” Her work reflects a strong commitment to advancing data-driven cancer research and inspiring the next generation of bioinformaticians.
In her departmental research, she explores bioactive compounds from Bangladeshi medicinal plants as environmentally friendly larvicidal agents, offering alternatives to conventional insecticides. This research is aligned with the One Health and microbiome framework and supports the development of safer, sustainable public health solutions, including mosquito repellents and nanoparticle-based applications.
In addition to her research activities, she has served in leadership roles, including President of ISCB RSG Bangladesh, and actively engages in academic outreach, mentoring, and educational initiatives.
She is committed to integrating computational and experimental strategies in drug discovery and molecular biology, with the goal of building a career that bridges biological sciences and computational approaches to advance translational biomedical research.

Key Contributions:
- Supports computational biology research projects.
- Creates tutorials and pipelines for protein modeling.
- Assists in training other students and researchers.
- Maintains reproducible code and workflow documentation

Key Takeaways:
- AlphaFold predicts protein structures with high accuracy.
- Useful for research in drug discovery, molecular biology, and bioinformatics.
- Free tools (AlphaFold DB and ColabFold) allow hands-on exploration.
- Understanding AlphaFold helps participants see the future of computational biology.

title: Courses
text: Scientific Sessions

title: AlphaFold – The Future of Structural Biology
text:
Overview:
AlphaFold is a breakthrough AI tool developed by DeepMind that predicts the 3D structures of proteins from their amino acid sequences. This technology has transformed structural biology, helping researchers understand protein folding, function, and interactions—key for drug design, protein engineering, and functional annotation.
This webinar introduced the basics of AlphaFold and showed its potential applications in research. Participants can explore AlphaFold further using the resources below.




AlphaFold Protein Structure Database (EMBL-EBI):
https://www.alphafold.ebi.ac.uk – Search and download predicted protein structures.
ColabFold (Google Colab notebook for predictions):
https://colab.research.google.com/github/sokrypton/ColabFold – Try predictions interactively.
YouTube Overview Video: AlphaFold: The making of a scientific breakthrough


Key Takeaways:
- AlphaFold predicts protein structures with high accuracy.
- Useful for research in drug discovery, molecular biology, and bioinformatics.
- Free tools (AlphaFold DB and ColabFold) allow hands-on exploration.
- Understanding AlphaFold helps participants see the future of computational biology.

title: Basic Research and Bioinformatics Training
text:
This course introduces foundational research skills and practical bioinformatics techniques, including scientific literature analysis, sequence exploration, and molecular visualization.



This training is designed for beginners and aspiring researchers who want to gain practical knowledge in basic research concepts and bioinformatics. Participants will learn how to approach scientific problems, analyze data, and apply computational tools to biological questions.





Research Fundamentals
Understanding research design, literature review, hypothesis formulation, and data interpretation.
Scientific Literature Analysis
How to search, read, and summarize scientific articles efficiently.
Bioinformatics Tools
Introduction to sequence retrieval, physicochemical analysis, subcellular localization, protein function prediction, and molecular visualization.
Practical Exercises
Hands-on training using real datasets to reinforce learning.
Data Management & Reporting
How to document findings and prepare reports for academic or research purposes.






Students of life sciences, biotechnology, or related fields

Beginners in bioinformatics and computational biology

Anyone interested in learning research methodology and practical bioinformatics skills





By the end of this training, participants will be able to:

Conduct literature searches and summarize findings effectively

Apply basic bioinformatics analyses on DNA, RNA, and protein sequences

Use molecular visualization tools to explore protein structures

Develop confidence in approaching small research projects





Drop a message on the page if you are interested in joining this course.

title: Integrating CADD & BioPython for Biomedical Research
text: title: “Integrating CADD & BioPython for Biomedical Research” description: “A hands-on course on computational drug design and Python-based bioinformatics for biomedical applications” —

title: Integrating CADD & BioPython for Biomedical Research
text:
📘 Course Overview
This course focuses on integrating Computational Drug Design (CADD) and Python programming (BioPython) to address biomedical research challenges. Participants will learn how to analyze protein structures, explore protein–ligand interactions, and apply computational workflows commonly used in modern drug discovery.
The training emphasizes hands-on learning, enabling students to connect computational tools with real biomedical research problems.

title: Integrating CADD & BioPython for Biomedical Research
text:
🧠 What You Will Learn

Computational Drug Design (CADD) Fundamentals
Introduction to molecular docking, drug-target interactions, and structure-based drug design concepts.
Protein Structure Analysis & Visualization
Understanding protein structures and visualizing biomolecular interactions using computational tools.
BioPython Programming
Practical Python-based bioinformatics for handling, analyzing, and processing biological datasets.
Integrated Research Workflows
Combining CADD and BioPython into a unified pipeline for biomedical research applications.
Data Analysis & Interpretation
Extracting meaningful insights from computational results and presenting them in a research context.

title: Integrating CADD & BioPython for Biomedical Research
text:
👥 Who Should Attend

Students in bioinformatics, biotechnology, pharmacy, and life sciences

Beginners in computational drug design and Python programming

Researchers interested in computational approaches to biomedical problems

title: Integrating CADD & BioPython for Biomedical Research
text:
🚀 Course Outcomes
By the end of this course, participants will be able to:

Apply CADD techniques to analyze protein–ligand interactions

Use BioPython for biological sequence and structural data analysis

Integrate computational tools into end-to-end biomedical research workflows

Develop problem-solving skills for drug discovery and bioinformatics research

title: Integrating CADD & BioPython for Biomedical Research
text:
📩 Contact
For enrollment or more information, please contact the course page.

title: Integrating CADD & BioPython for Biomedical Research

title: Integrating CADD & BioPython for Biomedical Research
text:
This course introduces an integrated approach to Computational Aided Drug Design (CADD) and BioPython programming to solve real-world biomedical research problems. Students will gain both conceptual understanding and practical skills in drug discovery and biological data analysis.



This course focuses on integrating Computational Drug Design (CADD) and Python programming (BioPython) to address biomedical research challenges. Participants will learn how to analyze protein structures, explore protein–ligand interactions, and apply computational workflows commonly used in modern drug discovery.
The training emphasizes hands-on learning, enabling students to connect computational tools with real biomedical research problems.





Computational Drug Design (CADD) Fundamentals
Introduction to molecular docking, drug-target interactions, and structure-based drug design concepts.
Protein Structure Analysis & Visualization
Understanding protein structures and visualizing biomolecular interactions using computational tools.
BioPython Programming
Practical Python-based bioinformatics for handling, analyzing, and processing biological datasets.
Integrated Research Workflows
Combining CADD and BioPython into a unified pipeline for biomedical research applications.
Data Analysis & Interpretation
Extracting meaningful insights from computational results and presenting them in a research context.






Students in bioinformatics, biotechnology, pharmacy, and life sciences

Beginners in computational drug design and Python programming

Researchers interested in computational approaches to biomedical problems





By the end of this course, participants will be able to:

Apply CADD techniques to analyze protein–ligand interactions

Use BioPython for biological sequence and structural data analysis

Integrate computational tools into end-to-end biomedical research workflows

Develop problem-solving skills for drug discovery and bioinformatics research





For enrollment or more information, please contact the course page.

title: Get Learn Bioinformatics
text:
FB Group
  
  
    
     
  
  
    
     
  

  
  

Welcome to Get Learn Bioinformatics
Get Learn Bioinformatics (GLB) is an educational and research-focused platform dedicated to making bioinformatics, computational biology, and research learning more accessible, practical, and understandable for students, beginners, and aspiring researchers.
We are committed to creating a learning environment where students can explore, practice, and grow in the field of bioinformatics with proper guidance and real-world application.
Who We Are: We are a research and education-focused platform dedicated to making bioinformatics and computational biology accessible for students, beginners, and aspiring researchers.
What We Do: Provide hands-on training in bioinformatics tools and molecular visualization Guide learners through research methodology and scientific literature analysis Offer workshops, mentorship, and academic support for research projects
Our Mission: To empower learners with practical skills and confidence to explore, analyze, and contribute to scientific research.
Our Vision: To create a community where students and researchers can grow, innovate, and succeed in bioinformatics and computational biology.
Learn. Explore. Research. Grow.
